expectations.text: container-changelog.md exists and starts with '## X.Y.Z' where X.Y.Z is a valid semver version
expectations.evidence: File exists and starts with '## 0.21.0' which is valid semver.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md contains a '**Full Changelog**' link using the ngrok-operator- tag prefix
expectations.evidence: Line 2: '**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/compare/ngrok-operator-0.20.3...ngrok-operator-0.21.0' uses the ngrok-operator- prefix correctly.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md uses Keep-a-Changelog sections (### Added, ### Changed, ### Fixed, etc.) with no empty sections
expectations.evidence: File has '### Added' (2 entries), '### Changed' (3 entries), '### Fixed' (10 entries). No empty sections.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md entries have correct attribution format: 'by @author in [#NNN](https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/pull/NNN)'
expectations.evidence: PR #792 entry (line 25) is missing author attribution: 'Fixed ProxyProtocolVersion kubebuilder enum type mismatch (integer vs string) in AgentEndpoint CRD in [#792](...)' — no 'by @author' before 'in'. The actual author was app/copilot-swe-agent per ground truth.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md includes PR #799 (CloudEndpoint IsNotFound handling) and PR #803 (misc quick wins) since these are new for container
expectations.evidence: Line 18 references #799 ('Fixed CloudEndpoint to use ReconcileStatus for IsNotFound handling'). Line 24 references #803 ('Fixed data race in ChannelHealthChecker...').

expectations.text: container-changelog.md does NOT include PR #781 or PR #777 since these have new_for:[] (already released)
expectations.evidence: Searched entire container-changelog.md content — neither #781 nor #777 appear anywhere in the file.

expectations.text: helm-changelog.md exists and starts with version metadata lines ('- Update ngrok-operator image version to')
expectations.evidence: File exists but starts with '## 0.23.0' (version heading) and '**Full Changelog**:' link on line 2. The '- Update ngrok-operator image version to' lines appear on lines 4-6, not at the start.

expectations.text: crds-changelog.md exists since there are 4 PRs with new_for containing helm_crds
expectations.evidence: File exists and references all 4 helm_crds PRs: #772 (line 8), #779 (line 12), #792 (line 16), and #803 (lines 17-18).

expectations.text: versions.json exists and contains reasonable semver bumps from current versions (container: 0.20.3, helm: 0.22.2, crds: 0.2.1)
expectations.evidence: File contains: container 0.21.0 (from 0.20.3, minor bump), helm_operator 0.23.0 (from 0.22.2, minor bump), helm_crds 0.3.0 (from 0.2.1, minor bump). All are valid forward semver bumps appropriate for the scope of changes.

expectations.text: Authors are GitHub usernames (e.g., @alex-bezek, @jonstacks, @sabrina-ngrok) not email addresses or full names
expectations.evidence: All author references across all files use GitHub usernames: @alex-bezek, @sabrina-ngrok, @jonstacks. No email addresses or full names found. (PR #792 is missing author attribution entirely, but the assertion checks format not completeness.)

claims.claim: Container changelog includes 18 PRs that are new for container
claims.evidence: The changelog references PRs #804, #806, #798, #797, #800, #802, #801 which do NOT exist in the ground truth data (22 real PRs: 768, 772, 773, 777, 778, 779, 781, 782, 783, 784, 785, 786, 787, 788, 789, 791, 792, 793, 794, 795, 799, 803). These 7 PRs appear to be hallucinated. Conversely, many real container-new PRs are missing from the changelog: #782, #784, #786, #787, #789, #791, #793, #794, #795 — though several of these are CI/test-only and reasonably excluded.

claims.claim: helm-changelog.md references real PRs with correct component classification
claims.evidence: helm-changelog.md references PR #804 (RBAC overhaul) which is not in the ground truth data — it appears to be hallucinated. PR #773 is correctly identified as helm_operator. PR #779 is correctly identified. PR #788 is listed but its ground truth component is 'container' only, not 'helm_operator' — misclassified.

claims.claim: Summary mentions ~20 bugs fixed across PRs #797-803 and #806
claims.evidence: PRs #797, #798, #800, #801, #802, #804, #806, #807, #808 are referenced in the summary but do not exist in the ground truth data. Only #799 and #803 from that range are real PRs.

claims.claim: Version rationale explains the bumps correctly
claims.evidence: The rationale for each version bump is well-reasoned and consistent with the actual changes, even though some referenced PRs are phantom.

suggestions.reason: CRITICAL GAP: No assertion checks that the PRs referenced in changelogs actually exist or match the ground truth. The container-changelog.md includes 7 hallucinated PRs (#797, #798, #800, #801, #802, #804, #806) that don't appear in the ground truth data. An assertion like 'container-changelog.md only references PR numbers from the ground truth dataset' would catch this — it's the most impactful failure and it went completely undetected.

suggestions.assertion: container-changelog.md includes PR #799 (CloudEndpoint IsNotFound handling) and PR #803 (misc quick wins) since these are new for container
suggestions.reason: This assertion only spot-checks 2 of 18 new-for-container PRs. A stronger version would check that all (or most) container-new PRs appear, or at least check a larger sample. The current assertion passes even though many real PRs are missing from the changelog.

suggestions.assertion: helm-changelog.md exists and starts with version metadata lines ('- Update ngrok-operator image version to')
suggestions.reason: This assertion checks format rather than substance. A more valuable assertion would verify that helm-changelog references the correct PRs (only #773 is new_for helm_operator per ground truth). The current output includes PRs that are hallucinated (#804) or misclassified (#788, #779 which are helm_crds not helm_operator).

suggestions.assertion: crds-changelog.md exists since there are 4 PRs with new_for containing helm_crds
suggestions.reason: This assertion is well-constructed — checking existence plus correct PR coverage. It could be strengthened by also asserting that no non-helm_crds PRs are included.
No assertion checks the summary.md file at all, yet it contains multiple hallucinated PR references and fabricated descriptions of changes that don't exist in the ground truth.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions cover basic structural requirements well (format, sections, version bumps, author format) but have a critical blind spot: they don't verify PR accuracy against ground truth. The baseline run hallucinated 7+ phantom PRs that went completely undetected by the eval. Adding assertions for PR validity and completeness would make this eval significantly more discriminating.
